snapshot.scope: Manual review binds public/documentation surfaces, control manifests, verifier code and shared executable schemas; automated gates execute current implementation evidence separately.

reviewers.task: macOS-first UI shell and progressive Models exact-candidate review
reviewers.independence: UI Design directly owned all Models renderer/locale bytes and accepted the exact combined 19-path Development hash. Planning reproduced both identities and reviewed only owner, platform, evidence and unchanged-status projections without editing frozen paths.

dimensions.summary: Existing UI, Technical Architecture, Quality and AI-development owners locate the progressive Models flow, shared typed ownership, macOS-first gate and deferred platform qualification.
Owners agree on UI/backend parallelism, mechanical integration, Global Default-first Models pages, existing registry/secret authority, macOS hiddenInset and no false Windows claim.
Progressive Models evidence is exact and separate from service capability; semantic lock v3 has 1,213 claims, while Windows visual qualification and every status stay open/unchanged.
Planning owns UI-centered requirements; UI Design and Development proceed in parallel from one contract, then Development binds mechanically and UI Design reviews exact bytes.
No document or decision is added; existing owner, visual and acceptance projections remain inside fixed budgets.

verification.evidence: The exact replacement candidate passes docs, decisions, UI visual, independent review, TRC-001 through TRC-005, CON-001 through CON-004, dependencies, fixtures, boundaries, no telemetry, source artifacts, LEN-001 through LEN-005 and all quality dimensions.

verification.command: Exact candidate focused and macOS Electron evidence
verification.evidence: Focused 6 files/102 tests, typecheck and build pass. Real macOS Electron proves hiddenInset plus Models overview, preset, Custom and Provider detail through the typed API; UI Design accepted the exact final hash.

verification.command: npm run verify:ui-visual && npm run verify:traceability && npm run verify:contracts
verification.evidence: The 544-combination visual contract passes; semantic lock v3/1,213 and CON-001 through CON-004 pass with Requirement, Exit and Phase states unchanged.
LEN-001 through LEN-005 pass within the fixed entry, trace and governed Markdown budgets.

coordination.taskTitle: macOS-first UI shell and progressive Models integration
coordination.compatibility: The candidate changes Models presentation over existing typed calls and the already bounded window adapter; no IPC, schema, dependency, provider, permission, secret or durable owner changes.
coordination.acknowledgement: Development froze exact 877405a0/c727e07c bytes after UI Design owned the Models production UI and accepted the combined candidate. Planning bound existing owners, visual contract, acceptance, semantic lock and this recipe without changing those bytes.

nonBlockingRisks: Replacement PR CI and macOS arm64 package/downloaded-distribution are the next complete node; tiered M verification intentionally did not repeat full local package/test gates.
Windows native titlebar qualification and its isolated evidence lane remain deferred; the coded adapter is not a Windows support claim.
Unavailable capabilities remain polished local development states, never executable feature or status evidence.
